mechanism_narrative: A sustained low unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates a strong and stable labor market, which supports robust consumer spending and corporate profitability. This economic strength translates into positive investor sentiment and drives S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Low unemployment typically correlates with economic expansion.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with UNRATE levels over a 5-year rolling window, or the average return in periods of low UNRATE is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: A significant month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) signals weakening consumer demand, which directly impacts corporate revenues and earnings expectations. This deterioration in fundamental outlook leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines by more than 2% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is a major component of GDP.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where RSAFS declines by more than 2% MoM, or the average return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is rising while the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) remains low, it indicates an accommodative monetary policy in the face of increasing prices. This environment can fuel asset price inflation by compressing discount rates and encouraging risk-taking, leading to positive S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.2% MoM for 3 consecutive months AND FEDFUNDS < 1.0%
evidence_refs: fact:Low interest rates reduce the cost of capital.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative or flat in periods where CPIAUCSL rises and FEDFUNDS remains low, failing to show a statistically significant positive return.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high unemployment (UNRATE), a significant increase in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals a robust recovery in the labor market. This boosts consumer confidence and corporate outlook, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns as investors anticipate stronger economic growth.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by more than 500,000 in a month AND UNRATE > 7%
evidence_refs: fact:Labor market recovery often precedes broader economic recovery.
failure_condition: In post-recession periods, large PAYEMS increases do not consistently lead to positive SPX 5d returns, or the effect is not statistically significant (p-value > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts (HOUST) indicates a significant slowdown in the construction sector and broader economic activity. This reflects reduced consumer and business confidence, which typically precedes negative S&P 500 returns due to anticipated declines in corporate earnings.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by more than 15% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Housing is a cyclical and leading sector of the economy.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in more than 40% of instances following a >15% MoM decline in HOUST, or the average return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in industrial production (INDPRO) after a period of contraction indicates a recovery in manufacturing and overall supply-side activity. This signals improving corporate earnings and capacity utilization, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns as economic activity accelerates.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO increases for two consecutive months after a prior 3-month decline
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production reflects the health of the manufacturing sector.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns do not show a statistically significant positive response to INDPRO rebounds, or the average return is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) signal a tightening monetary policy aimed at curbing inflation. This raises borrowing costs for corporations and consumers, negatively impacting earnings and economic growth, leading to lower S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.25% over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:Higher interest rates increase the cost of capital.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in more than 50% of periods following a significant FEDFUNDS increase, or the average return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates a robust and expanding economy, which translates into higher corporate revenues and profits. This fundamental improvement drives positive investor sentiment and S&P 500 forward returns as future earnings expectations are revised upwards.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth rate accelerates by more than 1 percentage point
evidence_refs: fact:GDP is the broadest measure of economic activity.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns do not show a statistically significant positive correlation with accelerating GDPC1 growth, or the average return is not positive.
